name: Is laminate or vinyl plank better for our coastal Bay Area home with kids and pets?
headline: Is laminate or vinyl plank better for our coastal Bay Area home with kids and pets?

mainEntity.name: Is laminate or vinyl plank better for our coastal Bay Area home with kids and pets?
mainEntity.text: Is laminate or vinyl plank better for our coastal Bay Area home with kids and pets?

acceptedAnswer.text: Both laminate and luxury vinyl plank (LVP) can work well in coastal California homes, but they have different strengths. LVP is usually the better choice for areas with moisture, like ground floors, basements, kitchens, and homes near the ocean. It is waterproof or very water-resistant, handles spills and pet accidents, and is more stable if humidity changes. It is also quieter and softer underfoot than laminate. Laminate can swell or warp if water gets into the seams, so it needs more care around spills. Laminate can look and feel more like real wood, and good-quality products are very scratch-resistant. If you do not have moisture problems and you want a more “wood-like” feel on a budget, laminate can be a great option. Just be careful with standing water and wet mopping. For both products, you need a flat, clean, and dry subfloor, and you must leave the required expansion gaps around walls and fixed objects so the floor can move without buckling. A common mistake is choosing based only on color or price. Think about where the floor goes (upstairs vs. slab), how your family uses the space (kids, pets), and how much water it might see. That will help you choose the right product for your home.
